compiled_state.title: [plugins/dso skills+prompts]: hardcoded `make` invocations bypass commands.* config -> plugin not stack-agnostic
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

Per CLAUDE.md "Stack-agnostic gate pipeline": all executable invocations of test/lint/format/type-check commands inside plugin skills, prompts, and agent files MUST resolve commands from the project's config (`commands.test`, `commands.test_unit`, `commands.test_e2e`, `commands.test_visual`, `commands.lint`, `commands.format`, `commands.format_check`, `commands.type_check`) rather than hardcoding `make` targets. The plugin is shipped to projects whose stacks may not use Make at all (e.g., `npm test`, `cargo test`, `poetry run pytest`). Hardcoded `make` calls fail silently or with a confusing "make: *** No rule to make target" error on those stacks. Project-relative working-directory prefixes (`cd app && ...`) are part of the same anti-pattern: the `app/` subdir is project-specific.

#### Actual Behavior

Five executable instruction sites hardcode `make` (and in most cases also hardcode `cd app && ...`):

1. `plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md:1015-1020` — Done-Definition checklist:
   - `make test-unit-only`, `make lint`, `make format-check`
   - all wrapped in `cd \$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/app && ...`
2. `plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/prompts/diagnostic-and-cluster.md:33-59` — diagnostic mappings:
   - `format` → `make format-check`
   - `ruff` → `make lint-ruff`
   - `mypy` → `make lint-mypy`
   - `tests` → `make test-unit-only`
   - `e2e` → `make test-e2e`
   - plus inline invocations on lines 47, 50, 53, 56, 59.
3. `plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/test-failure-fix.md:76` — full validation pipeline hardcoded:
   - \`{ cd \"\$_REPO/app\" && make format-check && make lint && make test-unit-only; } > \"\$_RESULT\" 2>&1\`
4. `plugins/dso/skills/validate-work/prompts/playwright-verification.md:65` — visual regression:
   - \`cd \$REPO_ROOT/app && make test-visual\`
5. `plugins/dso/skills/interface-contracts/SKILL.md:161, 171, 181` — hardcoded \`make lint-mypy\` as the type-check verification command.

#### Acceptable / illustrative usages (NOT in scope)

- \`plugins/dso/docs/dso-config.example.conf\`, \`docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md\`, \`docs/MIGRATION-TO-PLUGIN.md\`, \`docs/PRE-COMMIT-TIMEOUT-WRAPPER.md\` — all documentation that surfaces \`make\` as one example accepted value alongside other stack examples.
- \`plugins/dso/agents/code-reviewer-*.md:53\` — appears in a "Do NOT run e.g. \`make test\`" prohibition; illustrative, not invoking.
- \`plugins/dso/skills/init/SKILL.md\` — stack-detection presets surface \`make\` as defaults for python-poetry, for user confirmation.
- \`plugins/dso/skills/retro/SKILL.md\`, \`debug-everything/SKILL.md:519\` — table/prose mentions in narrative documentation.

### 5. Skills and Workflows

- \`/dso:implementation-plan\` (Done Definition checklist)
- \`/dso:debug-everything\` (diagnostic + cluster prompts)
- \`/dso:validate-work\` (playwright verification)
- \`/dso:interface-contracts\` (mypy verification step)
- \`shared/prompts/test-failure-fix.md\` (consumed by sprint, fix-bug)

### 6. Logs

Discovered via:
\`\`\`
grep -rn "make test\|make format\|make lint\|make ci" plugins/dso/skills/ plugins/dso/scripts/ plugins/dso/hooks/
\`\`\`

#### Suggested Remediation

Replace each hardcoded invocation with a config-resolved one:
\`\`\`bash
TEST_UNIT=\$(.claude/scripts/dso read-config commands.test_unit)
LINT=\$(.claude/scripts/dso read-config commands.lint)
FORMAT_CHECK=\$(.claude/scripts/dso read-config commands.format_check)
\$TEST_UNIT
\`\`\`
Working-directory prefixes (\`cd app && ...\`) should likewise resolve from config (\`commands.cwd\` or equivalent) — or be removed when the consuming project runs commands from the repo root.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral — skill/prompt files hardcode make commands instead of reading from commands.* config
comments.author: Test
